top_categories.workout_context: 5,906ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 99, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Atlas Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
60ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Atlas Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 99 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Atlas Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 99 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,906ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 99 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Atlas Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Tempo is Atlas Gran Fondo's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 99 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 99 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Atlas Gran Fondo’s 99 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Atlas Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 99 miles of mixed terrain in Taroudant, Morocco.
